members.name: AutoFilter
members.signature: AutoFilter([Field As Variant], [Criteria1 As Variant], [Operator As XlAutoFilterOperator], [Criteria2 As Variant], [VisibleDropDown As Variant], [SubField As Variant]) As Variant

doc.summary: Filters a list by using the AutoFilter.

params.name: Field
params.text: The integer offset of the field on which you want to base the filter (from the left of the list; the leftmost field is field one).

params.name: Criteria1
params.text: The criteria (a string; for example, "101"). Use "=" to find blank fields, "<>" to find non-blank fields, and "><" to select (No Data) fields in data types. If this argument is omitted, the criteria is All. If _Operator_ is xlTop10Items, _Criteria1_ specifies the number of items (for example, "10").

params.name: Operator
params.text: An XlAutoFilterOperator constant specifying the type of filter.

params.name: Criteria2
params.text: The second criteria (a string). Used with _Criteria1_ and _Operator_ to construct compound criteria. Also used as single criteria on date fields filtering by date, month or year. Followed by an Array detailing the filtering Array(Level, Date). Where Level is 0-2 (year,month,date) and Date is one valid Date inside the filtering period.

params.name: VisibleDropDown
params.text: True to display the AutoFilter drop-down arrow for the filtered field. False to hide the AutoFilter drop-down arrow for the filtered field. True by default.

params.name: SubField
params.text: The field from a data type on which to apply the criteria (for example, the "Population" field from Geography or "Volume" field from Stocks). Omitting this value targets the "(Display Value)".

doc.summary: Changes the width of the columns in the range or the height of the rows in the range to achieve the best fit.

members.name: BorderAround
members.signature: BorderAround([LineStyle As Variant], [Weight As XlBorderWeight], [ColorIndex As XlColorIndex], [Color As Variant], [ThemeColor As Variant]) As Variant

doc.summary: Adds a border to a range and sets the Color, LineStyle, and Weight properties of the Border object for the new border. Variant.

params.name: LineStyle
params.text: One of the constants of XlLineStyle specifying the line style for the border.

params.name: Weight
params.text: The border weight.

params.name: ColorIndex
params.text: The border color, as an index into the current color palette or as an XlColorIndex constant.

params.name: Color
params.text: The border color, as an RGB value.

params.name: ThemeColor
params.text: The theme color, as an index into the current color theme or as an XlThemeColor value.

doc.summary: Calculates all open workbooks, a specific worksheet in a workbook, or a specified range of cells on a worksheet, as shown in the table in the Remarks section.
Clears the entire object.
Clears formulas and values from the range.
Clears the formatting of the object.
Copies the range to the specified range or to the Clipboard.

params.name: Destination
params.text: Specifies the new range to which the specified range will be copied. If this argument is omitted, Microsoft Excel copies the range to the Clipboard.

doc.summary: Cuts the object to the Clipboard or pastes it into a specified destination.

params.name: Destination
params.text: The range where the object should be pasted. If this argument is omitted, the object is cut to the Clipboard.

doc.summary: Deletes the object.

params.name: Shift
params.text: Used only with Range objects. Specifies how to shift cells to replace deleted cells. Can be one of the following XlDeleteShiftDirection constants: xlShiftToLeft or xlShiftUp. If this argument is omitted, Microsoft Excel decides based on the shape of the range.

members.name: Find
members.signature: Find(What As Variant, [After As Variant], [LookIn As Variant], [LookAt As Variant], [SearchOrder As Variant], [SearchDirection As XlSearchDirection], [MatchCase As Variant], [MatchByte As Variant], [SearchFormat As Variant]) As Range

doc.summary: Finds specific information in a range.

params.name: What
params.text: The data to search for. Can be a string or any Microsoft Excel data type.

params.name: After
params.text: The cell after which you want the search to begin. This corresponds to the position of the active cell when a search is done from the user interface. Notice that _After_ must be a single cell in the range. Remember that the search begins after this cell; the specified cell isn't searched until the method wraps back around to this cell. If you don't specify this argument, the search starts after the cell in the upper-left corner of the range.

params.name: LookIn
params.text: Can be one of the following XlFindLookIn constants: xlFormulas, xlValues, xlComments, or xlCommentsThreaded.

params.name: LookAt
params.text: Can be one of the following XlLookAt constants: xlWhole or xlPart.

params.name: SearchOrder
params.text: Can be one of the following XlSearchOrder constants: xlByRows or xlByColumns.

params.name: SearchDirection
params.text: Can be one of the following XlSearchDirection constants: xlNext or xlPrevious.

params.name: MatchCase
params.text: True to make the search case-sensitive. The default value is False.

params.name: MatchByte
params.text: Used only if you have selected or installed double-byte language support. True to have double-byte characters match only double-byte characters. False to have double-byte characters match their single-byte equivalents.

params.name: SearchFormat
params.text: The search format.

doc.summary: Continues a search that was begun with the Find method. Finds the next cell that matches those same conditions and returns a Range object that represents that cell. This does not affect the selection or the active cell.

params.name: After
params.text: The cell after which you want to search. This corresponds to the position of the active cell when a search is done from the user interface. Be aware that _After_ must be a single cell in the range.

members.name: Insert
members.signature: Insert([Shift As Variant], [CopyOrigin As Variant]) As Variant

doc.summary: Inserts a cell or a range of cells into the worksheet or macro sheet and shifts other cells away to make space.

params.name: Shift
params.text: Specifies which way to shift the cells. Can be one of the following XlInsertShiftDirection constants: xlShiftToRight or xlShiftDown. If this argument is omitted, Microsoft Excel decides based on the shape of the range.

params.name: CopyOrigin
params.text: The copy origin; that is, from where to copy the format for inserted cells. Can be one of the following XlInsertFormatOrigin constants: xlFormatFromLeftOrAbove (default) or xlFormatFromRightOrBelow.

doc.summary: Creates a merged cell from the specified Range object.

params.name: Across
params.text: True to merge cells in each row of the specified range as separate merged cells. The default value is False.

members.name: PasteSpecial
members.signature: PasteSpecial([Paste As XlPasteType], [Operation As XlPasteSpecialOperation], [SkipBlanks As Variant], [Transpose As Variant]) As Variant

doc.summary: Pastes a Range object that has been copied into the specified range.

params.name: Paste
params.text: The part of the range to be pasted, such as xlPasteAll or xlPasteValues.

params.name: Operation
params.text: The paste operation, such as xlPasteSpecialOperationAdd.

params.name: SkipBlanks
params.text: True to have blank cells in the range on the clipboard not be pasted into the destination range. The default value is False.

params.name: Transpose
params.text: True to transpose rows and columns when the range is pasted. The default value is False.

members.name: Replace
members.signature: Replace(What As Variant, Replacement As Variant, [LookAt As Variant], [SearchOrder As Variant], [MatchCase As Variant], [MatchByte As Variant], [SearchFormat As Variant], [ReplaceFormat As Variant], [FormulaVersion As Variant]) As Boolean

doc.summary: Returns a Boolean indicating characters in cells within the specified range. Using this method doesn't change either the selection or the active cell.

params.name: What
params.text: The string that you want Microsoft Excel to search for.

params.name: Replacement
params.text: The replacement string.

params.name: LookAt
params.text: Can be one of the following XlLookAt constants: xlWhole or xlPart.

params.name: SearchOrder
params.text: Can be one of the following XlSearchOrder constants: xlByRows or xlByColumns.

params.name: MatchCase
params.text: True to make the search case-sensitive.

params.name: MatchByte
params.text: Use this argument only if you have selected or installed double-byte language support in Microsoft Excel. True to have double-byte characters match only double-byte characters. False to have double-byte characters match their single-byte equivalents.

params.name: SearchFormat
params.text: The search format for the method.

params.name: ReplaceFormat
params.text: The replace format for the method.

params.name: FormulaVersion
params.text: Determines whether to replace in Dynamic Array formula state constants: xlReplaceFormula or xlReplaceFormula2.

doc.summary: Selects the object.

members.name: Sort
members.signature: Sort([Key1 As Variant], [Order1 As XlSortOrder], [Key2 As Variant], [Type As Variant], [Order2 As XlSortOrder], [Key3 As Variant], [Order3 As XlSortOrder], [Header As XlYesNoGuess], [OrderCustom As Variant], [MatchCase As Variant], [Orientation As XlSortOrientation], [SortMethod As XlSortMethod], [DataOption1 As XlSortDataOption], [DataOption2 As XlSortDataOption], [DataOption3 As XlSortDataOption], [SubField1 As Variant]) As Variant

doc.summary: Sorts a range of values.

params.name: Key1
params.text: Specifies the first sort field, either as a range name (String) or Range object; determines the values to be sorted.

params.name: Order1
params.text: Determines the sort order for the values specified in _Key1_.

params.name: Key2
params.text: Second sort field; cannot be used when sorting a PivotTable.

params.name: Type
params.text: Specifies which type of elements are to be sorted within a PivotTable. Specify xLSortLabels to sort by the labels targeted by the _experession_ Range in the Row/Colum Labels areas of the PivotTable's range, or xLSortValues when _Key1_ additionally targets a cell in the Values or Sub/Grand Totals areas using R1C1 notation.

params.name: Order2
params.text: Determines the sort order for the values specified in _Key2_.

params.name: Key3
params.text: Third sort field; cannot be used when sorting a PivotTable.

params.name: Order3
params.text: Determines the sort order for the values specified in _Key3_.

params.name: Header
params.text: Specifies whether the first row contains header information. xlNo is the default value; specify xlGuess if you want Excel to attempt to determine the header.

params.name: OrderCustom
params.text: Specifies a one-based integer offset into the list of custom sort orders.

params.name: MatchCase
params.text: Set to True to perform a case-sensitive sort, False to perform a non-case-sensitive sort; cannot be used with PivotTables.

params.name: Orientation
params.text: Specifies if the sort should be by row (default) or column. Set xlSortColumns value to 1 to sort by column. Set xlSortRows value to 2 to sort by row (this is the default value).

params.name: SortMethod
params.text: Specifies the sort method.

params.name: DataOption1
params.text: Specifies how to sort text in the range specified in _Key1_; does not apply to PivotTable sorting.

params.name: DataOption2
params.text: Specifies how to sort text in the range specified in _Key2_; does not apply to PivotTable sorting.

params.name: DataOption3
params.text: Specifies how to sort text in the range specified in _Key3_; does not apply to PivotTable sorting.

members.name: SpecialCells
members.signature: SpecialCells(Type As XlCellType, [Value As Variant]) As Range

doc.summary: Returns a Range object that represents all the cells that match the specified type and value.

params.name: Type
params.text: The cells to include.

params.name: Value
params.text: If _Type_ is either xlCellTypeConstants or xlCellTypeFormulas, this argument is used to determine which types of cells to include in the result. These values can be added together to return more than one type. The default is to select all constants or formulas, no matter what the type.

members.name: TextToColumns
members.signature: TextToColumns([Destination As Variant], [DataType As XlTextParsingType], [TextQualifier As XlTextQualifier], [ConsecutiveDelimiter As Variant], [Tab As Variant], [Semicolon As Variant], [Comma As Variant], [Space As Variant], [Other As Variant], [OtherChar As Variant], [FieldInfo As Variant], [DecimalSeparator As Variant], [ThousandsSeparator As Variant], [TrailingMinusNumbers As Variant]) As Variant

doc.summary: Parses a column of cells that contain text into several columns.

params.name: Destination
params.text: A Range object that specifies where Microsoft Excel will place the results. If the range is larger than a single cell, the top left cell is used.

params.name: DataType
params.text: The format of the text to be split into columns.

params.name: TextQualifier
params.text: Specifies whether to use single, double, or no quotes as the text qualifier.

params.name: ConsecutiveDelimiter
params.text: True to have Excel consider consecutive delimiters as one delimiter. The default value is False.

params.name: Tab
params.text: True to have _DataType_ be xlDelimited and to have the tab character be a delimiter. The default value is False.

params.name: Semicolon
params.text: True to have _DataType_ be xlDelimited and to have the semicolon be a delimiter. The default value is False.

params.name: Comma
params.text: True to have _DataType_ be xlDelimited and to have the comma be a delimiter. The default value is False.

params.name: Space
params.text: True to have _DataType_ be xlDelimited and to have the space character be a delimiter. The default value is False.

params.name: Other
params.text: True to have _DataType_ be xlDelimited and to have the character specified by the _OtherChar_ argument be a delimiter. The default value is False.

params.name: OtherChar
params.text: Required if _Other_ is True; the delimiter character when _Other_ is True. If more than one character is specified, only the first character of the string is used; the remaining characters are ignored.

params.name: FieldInfo
params.text: An array containing parse information for the individual columns of data. The interpretation depends on the value of _DataType_. When the data is delimited, this argument is an array of two-element arrays, with each two-element array specifying the conversion options for a particular column. The first element is the column number (1-based), and the second element is one of the XlColumnDataType constants specifying how the column is parsed.

params.name: DecimalSeparator
params.text: The decimal separator that Microsoft Excel uses when recognizing numbers. The default setting is the system setting.

params.name: ThousandsSeparator
params.text: The thousands separator that Excel uses when recognizing numbers. The default setting is the system setting.

params.name: TrailingMinusNumbers
params.text: Numbers that begin with a minus character.

doc.summary: Separates a merged area into individual cells.
Returns or sets a Variant value that indicates if text is automatically indented when the text alignment in a cell is set to equal distribution (either horizontally or vertically).
Returns the range reference for the specified range in the language of the user. Read-only String.
Performs a writeback operation for all edited cells in a range based on an OLAP data source.
Returns a Boolean value that indicates if the range can be edited on a protected worksheet.

members.name: ApplyNames
members.signature: ApplyNames([Names As Variant], [IgnoreRelativeAbsolute As Variant], [UseRowColumnNames As Variant], [OmitColumn As Variant], [OmitRow As Variant], [Order As XlApplyNamesOrder], [AppendLast As Variant]) As Variant

doc.summary: Applies names to the cells in the specified range.

params.name: Names
params.text: An array of the names to be applied. If this argument is omitted, all names on the sheet are applied to the range.

params.name: IgnoreRelativeAbsolute
params.text: True to replace references with names, regardless of the reference types of either the names or references. False to replace absolute references only with absolute names, relative references only with relative names, and mixed references only with mixed names. The default value is True.

params.name: UseRowColumnNames
params.text: True to use the names of row and column ranges that contain the specified range if names for the range cannot be found. False to ignore the _OmitColumn_ and _OmitRow_ arguments. The default value is True.

params.name: OmitColumn
params.text: True to replace the entire reference with the row-oriented name. The column-oriented name can be omitted only if the referenced cell is in the same column as the formula and is within a row-oriented named range. The default value is True.

params.name: OmitRow
params.text: True to replace the entire reference with the column-oriented name. The row-oriented name can be omitted only if the referenced cell is in the same row as the formula and is within a column-oriented named range. The default value is True.

params.name: Order
params.text: Determines which range name is listed first when a cell reference is replaced by a row-oriented and column-oriented range name.

params.name: AppendLast
params.text: True to replace the definitions of the names in _Names_ and also replace the definitions of the last names that were defined. False to replace the definitions of the names in _Names_ only. The default value is False.

doc.summary: Applies outlining styles to the specified range.
Returns an AutoComplete match from the list. If there's no AutoComplete match or if more than one entry in the list matches the string to complete, this method returns an empty string.

params.name: String
params.text: The string to complete.

doc.summary: Automatically creates an outline for the specified range. If the range is a single cell, Microsoft Excel creates an outline for the entire sheet. The new outline replaces any existing outline.
Calculates a specified range of cells.
This is a child of the Range object, representing the cell controls contained within that range. Properties and methods here let you inspect and modify the cell controls on the range.
Returns a Characters object that represents a range of characters within the object text. Use the Characters object to format characters within a text string.

members.name: CheckSpelling
members.signature: CheckSpelling([CustomDictionary As Variant], [IgnoreUppercase As Variant], [AlwaysSuggest As Variant], [SpellLang As Variant]) As Variant

doc.summary: Checks the spelling of an object.

params.name: CustomDictionary
params.text: A string that indicates the file name of the custom dictionary to be examined if the word isn't found in the main dictionary. If this argument is omitted, the currently specified dictionary is used.

params.name: IgnoreUppercase
params.text: True to have Microsoft Excel ignore words that are all uppercase. False to have Excel check words that are all uppercase. If this argument is omitted, the current setting will be used.

params.name: AlwaysSuggest
params.text: True to have Excel display a list of suggested alternate spellings when an incorrect spelling is found. False to have Excel wait for you to input the correct spelling. If this argument is omitted, the current setting will be used.

params.name: SpellLang
params.text: The language of the dictionary being used. Can be one of the MsoLanguageID values.

doc.summary: Clears all cell comments from the specified range.
Removes all hyperlinks from the specified range.
Clears notes and sound notes from all the cells in the specified range.
Clears the outline for the specified range.
Returns a Range object that represents all the cells whose contents are different from the comparison cell in each column.

params.name: Comparison
params.text: A single cell to compare to the specified range.

members.name: Consolidate
members.signature: Consolidate([Sources As Variant], [Function As Variant], [TopRow As Variant], [LeftColumn As Variant], [CreateLinks As Variant]) As Variant

doc.summary: Consolidates data from multiple ranges on multiple worksheets into a single range on a single worksheet. Variant.

params.name: Sources
params.text: The sources of the consolidation as an array of text reference strings in R1C1-style notation. The references must include the full path of sheets to be consolidated.

params.name: Function
params.text: One of the constants of XlConsolidationFunction, which specifies the type of consolidation.

params.name: TopRow
params.text: True to consolidate data based on column titles in the top row of the consolidation ranges. False to consolidate data by position. The default value is False.

params.name: LeftColumn
params.text: True to consolidate data based on row titles in the left column of the consolidation ranges. False to consolidate data by position. The default value is False.

params.name: CreateLinks
params.text: True to have the consolidation use worksheet links. False to have the consolidation copy the data. The default value is False.

members.name: ConvertToLinkedDataType
members.signature: ConvertToLinkedDataType(ServiceID As Long, LanguageCulture As String)

doc.summary: Attempts to convert all the cells in the range to a Linked data type such as Stocks or Geography.

params.name: ServiceID
params.text: The ID of the service that will provide the linked entity.

params.name: LanguageCulture
params.text: A string representing the LCID of the language and culture that you would like to use for the linked entity.

members.name: CopyFromRecordset
members.signature: CopyFromRecordset(Data As IUnknown, [MaxRows As Variant], [MaxColumns As Variant]) As Long

doc.summary: Copies the contents of an ADO or DAO Recordset object onto a worksheet, beginning at the upper-left corner of the specified range. If the Recordset object contains fields with OLE objects in them, this method fails.

params.name: Data
params.text: The Recordset object to copy into the range.

params.name: MaxRows
params.text: The maximum number of records to copy onto the worksheet. If this argument is omitted, all the records in the Recordset object are copied.

params.name: MaxColumns
params.text: The maximum number of fields to copy onto the worksheet. If this argument is omitted, all the fields in the Recordset object are copied.

members.name: CopyPicture
members.signature: CopyPicture([Appearance As XlPictureAppearance], [Format As XlCopyPictureFormat]) As Variant

doc.summary: Copies the selected object to the Clipboard as a picture. Variant.

params.name: Appearance
params.text: Specifies how the picture should be copied.

params.name: Format
params.text: The format of the picture.

doc.summary: Returns a value that represents the number of objects in the collection. Read-only Variant.

members.name: CreateNames
members.signature: CreateNames([Top As Variant], [Left As Variant], [Bottom As Variant], [Right As Variant]) As Variant

doc.summary: Creates names in the specified range, based on text labels in the sheet.

params.name: Top
params.text: True to create names by using labels in the top row. The default value is False.

params.name: Left
params.text: True to create names by using labels in the left column. The default value is False.

params.name: Bottom
params.text: True to create names by using labels in the bottom row. The default value is False.

params.name: Right
params.text: True to create names by using labels in the right column. The default value is False.

doc.summary: Returns a 32-bit integer that indicates the application in which this object was created. Read-only Long.
If the specified cell is part of an array, returns a Range object that represents the entire array. Read-only.

members.name: DataSeries
members.signature: DataSeries([Rowcol As Variant], [Type As XlDataSeriesType], [Date As XlDataSeriesDate], [Step As Variant], [Stop As Variant], [Trend As Variant]) As Variant

doc.summary: Creates a data series in the specified range. Variant.

params.name: Rowcol
params.text: Can be the xlRows or xlColumns constant to have the data series entered in rows or columns, respectively. If this argument is omitted, the size and shape of the range is used.

params.name: Type
params.text: The type for the data series.

params.name: Date
params.text: If the _Type_ argument is xlChronological, the _Date_ argument indicates the step date unit.

params.name: Step
params.text: The step value for the series. The default value is 1.

params.name: Stop
params.text: The stop value for the series. If this argument is omitted, Microsoft Excel fills to the end of the range.

params.name: Trend
params.text: True to create a linear trend or growth trend. False to create a standard data series. The default value is False.

doc.summary: If any of the cells in the range are a Linked data type, such as Stocks or Geography, this call will convert their values to text.

Returns a Range object that represents the range containing all the dependents of a cell. This can be a multiple selection (a union of Range objects) if there's more than one dependent. Read-only Range object.
Displays a dialog box defined by a dialog box definition table on a Microsoft Excel 4.0 macro sheet. Returns the number of the chosen control, or returns False if the user chooses the Cancel button.

Returns a Range object that represents the range containing all the direct dependents of a cell. This can be a multiple selection (a union of Range objects) if there's more than one dependent. Read-only Range object.

Returns a Range object that represents the range containing all the direct precedents of a cell. This can be a multiple selection (a union of Range objects) if there's more than one precedent. Read-only Range object.
Designates a range to be recalculated when the next recalculation occurs.
Discards all changes in the edited cells of the range.

members.signature: EditionOptions(Type As XlEditionType, Option As XlEditionOptionsOption, [Name As Variant], [Reference As Variant], [Appearance As XlPictureAppearance], [ChartSize As XlPictureAppearance], [Format As Variant]) As Variant

members.name: ExportAsFixedFormat
members.signature: ExportAsFixedFormat(Type As XlFixedFormatType, [Filename As Variant], [Quality As Variant], [IncludeDocProperties As Variant], [IgnorePrintAreas As Variant], [From As Variant], [To As Variant], [OpenAfterPublish As Variant], [FixedFormatExtClassPtr As Variant], [WorkIdentity As Variant])

doc.summary: Exports to a file of the specified format.

params.name: Type
params.text: The type of file format to export to.

params.name: Filename
params.text: The file name of the file to be saved. You can include a full path, or Excel saves the file in the current folder.

params.name: Quality
params.text: Optional XlFixedFormatQuality. Specifies the quality of the published file.

params.name: IncludeDocProperties
params.text: True to include the document properties; otherwise, False.

params.name: IgnorePrintAreas
params.text: True to ignore any print areas set when publishing; otherwise, False.

params.name: From
params.text: The number of the page at which to start publishing. If this argument is omitted, publishing starts at the beginning.

params.name: To
params.text: The number of the last page to publish. If this argument is omitted, publishing ends with the last page.

params.name: OpenAfterPublish
params.text: True to display the file in the viewer after it is published; otherwise, False.

params.name: FixedFormatExtClassPtr
params.text: Pointer to the FixedFormatExt class.

doc.summary: Fills down from the top cell or cells in the specified range to the bottom of the range. The contents and formatting of the cell or cells in the top row of a range are copied into the rest of the rows in the range.

Fills left from the rightmost cell or cells in the specified range. The contents and formatting of the cell or cells in the rightmost column of a range are copied into the rest of the columns in the range.

Fills right from the leftmost cell or cells in the specified range. The contents and formatting of the cell or cells in the leftmost column of a range are copied into the rest of the columns in the range.

Fills up from the bottom cell or cells in the specified range to the top of the range. The contents and formatting of the cell or cells in the bottom row of a range are copied into the rest of the rows in the range.

Continues a search that was begun with the Find method. Finds the previous cell that matches those same conditions and returns a Range object that represents that cell. Doesn't affect the selection or the active cell.
True indicates that the Excel Flash Fill feature has been enabled and is active.

This is the Formula2 Variant of Range.FormulaLocal. See Range.Formula2 for more detail.
This is the Formula2 Variant of Range.FormulaR1C1. See Range.Formula2 and Range.FormulaR1C1 for more detail.
This is the Formula2 Variant of Range.FormulaR1C1Local. See Range.Formula2 and Range.FormulaR1C1Local for more detail.

Returns or sets the array formula of a range. Returns (or can be set to) a single formula or a Visual Basic array. If the specified range doesn't contain an array formula, this property returns null. Read/write Variant.
Returns or sets a Variant value that indicates if the formula will be hidden when the worksheet is protected.
Returns or sets the formula for the object, using A1-style references in the language of the user. Read/write Variant.
Returns or sets the formula for the object, using R1C1-style notation in the language of the user. Read/write Variant.
Starts the Function Wizard for the upper-left cell of the range.

members.name: Group
members.signature: Group([Start As Variant], [End As Variant], [By As Variant], [Periods As Variant]) As Variant

doc.summary: When the Range object represents a single cell in a PivotTable field's data range, the Group method performs numeric or date-based grouping in that field.

params.name: Start
params.text: The first value to be grouped. If this argument is omitted or True, the first value in the field is used.

params.name: End
params.text: The last value to be grouped. If this argument is omitted or True, the last value in the field is used.

params.name: By
params.text: If the field is numeric, this argument specifies the size of each group. If the field is a date, this argument specifies the number of days in each group if element 4 in the _Periods_ array is True and all the other elements are False. Otherwise, this argument is ignored. If this argument is omitted, Microsoft Excel automatically chooses a default group size.

params.name: Periods
params.text: An array of Boolean values that specify the period for the group, described in the Remarks section. If an element in the array is True, a group is created for the corresponding time; if the element is False, no group is created. If the field isn't a date field, this argument is ignored.

doc.summary: True if the specified cell is part of an array formula. Read-only Variant.
True if all cells in the range contain formulas; False if none of the cells in the range contains a formula; null otherwise. Read-only Variant.
True if all cells in the range contain a Rich data type. False if none of the cells in the range contains a Rich data type; otherwise, null. Read-only Variant.
True if all of the cells in the range are part of a spilled range; False if none of the cells in the range are part of a spilled range; null otherwise. Read-only Variant.
Returns or sets a String value that represents the identifying label for the specified cell when the page is saved as a webpage.
Returns or sets a Variant value that represents the indent level for the cell or range. Can be an integer from 0 to 15.
Adds an indent to the specified range.

params.name: InsertAmount
params.text: The amount to be added to the current indent.

doc.summary: Rearranges the text in a range so that it fills the range evenly.
Returns information about the state of any Linked data types, such as Stocks or Geography, in the range. Possible values are from the XlLinkedDataTypeState enumeration. Read-only.
Returns the number of header rows for the specified range. Read-only Long.
Pastes a list of all nonhidden names onto the worksheet, beginning with the first cell in the range.

Returns a constant that describes the part of the PivotTable report that contains the upper-left corner of the specified range. Can be one of the following XlLocationInTable constants. Read-only Long.
Returns the MDX name for the specified Range object. Read-only String.

members.name: NavigateArrow
members.signature: NavigateArrow([TowardPrecedent As Variant], [ArrowNumber As Variant], [LinkNumber As Variant]) As Variant

doc.summary: Navigates a tracer arrow for the specified range to the precedent, dependent, or error-causing cell or cells. Selects the precedent, dependent, or error cells and returns a Range object that represents the new selection. This method causes an error if it's applied to a cell without visible tracer arrows.

params.name: TowardPrecedent
params.text: Specifies the direction to navigate; True to navigate toward precedents, False to navigate toward dependents.

params.name: ArrowNumber
params.text: Specifies the arrow number to navigate; corresponds to the numbered reference in the cell's formula.

params.name: LinkNumber
params.text: If the arrow is an external reference arrow, this argument indicates which external reference to follow. If this argument is omitted, the first external reference is followed.

members.name: NoteText
members.signature: NoteText([Text As Variant], [Start As Variant], [Length As Variant]) As String

doc.summary: Returns or sets the cell note associated with the cell in the upper-left corner of the range. Read/write String. Cell notes have been replaced by range comments. For more information, see the Comment object.

params.name: Text
params.text: The text to add to the note (up to 255 characters). The text is inserted starting at position _Start_, replacing _Length_ characters of the existing note. If this argument is omitted, this method returns the current text of the note starting at position _Start_, for _Length_ characters.

params.name: Start
params.text: The starting position for the text that's set or returned. If this argument is omitted, this method starts at the first character. To append text to the note, specify a number larger than the number of characters in the existing note.

params.name: Length
params.text: The number of characters to be set or returned. If this argument is omitted, Microsoft Excel sets or returns characters from the starting position to the end of the note (up to 255 characters). If there are more than 255 characters from _Start_ to the end of the note, this method returns only 255 characters.

doc.summary: Returns or sets a Variant value that represents the format code for the object as a string in the language of the user.
Returns or sets the current outline level of the specified row or column. Read/write Variant.
Returns or sets the location of a page break. Can be one of the following XlPageBreak constants: xlPageBreakAutomatic, xlPageBreakManual, or xlPageBreakNone. Read/write Long.

members.name: Parse
members.signature: Parse([ParseLine As Variant], [Destination As Variant]) As Variant

doc.summary: Parses a range of data and breaks it into multiple cells. Distributes the contents of the range to fill several adjacent columns; the range can be no more than one column wide.

params.name: ParseLine
params.text: A string that contains left and right brackets to indicate where the cells should be split.

params.name: Destination
params.text: A Range object that represents the upper-left corner of the destination range for the parsed data. If this argument is omitted, Microsoft Excel parses in place.

doc.summary: Returns the Phonetic object, which contains information about a specific phonetic text string in a cell.
Returns the Phonetics collection of the range. Read-only.
Returns a PivotCell object that represents a cell in a PivotTable report.
Returns a PivotField object that represents the PivotTable field containing the upper-left corner of the specified range.
Returns a PivotItem object that represents the PivotTable item containing the upper-left corner of the specified range.
Returns a PivotTable object that represents the PivotTable report containing the upper-left corner of the specified range.
Returns a Range object that represents all the precedents of a cell. This can be a multiple selection (a union of Range objects) if there's more than one precedent. Read-only.
Returns the prefix character for the cell. Read-only Variant.

members.name: PrintOut
members.signature: PrintOut([From As Variant], [To As Variant], [Copies As Variant], [Preview As Variant], [ActivePrinter As Variant], [PrintToFile As Variant], [Collate As Variant], [PrToFileName As Variant]) As Variant

doc.summary: Prints the object.

params.name: From
params.text: The number of the page at which to start printing. If this argument is omitted, printing starts at the beginning.

params.name: To
params.text: The number of the last page to print. If this argument is omitted, printing ends with the last page.

params.name: Copies
params.text: The number of copies to print. If this argument is omitted, one copy is printed.

params.name: Preview
params.text: True to have Microsoft Excel invoke print preview before printing the object. False (or omitted) to print the object immediately.

params.name: ActivePrinter
params.text: Sets the name of the active printer.

params.name: PrintToFile
params.text: True to print to a file. If _PrToFileName_ is not specified, Microsoft Excel prompts the user to enter the name of the output file.

params.name: Collate
params.text: True to collate multiple copies.

params.name: PrToFileName
params.text: If _PrintToFile_ is set to True, this argument specifies the name of the file that you want to print to.

doc.summary: Shows a preview of the object as it would look when printed.

params.name: EnableChanges
params.text: Pass a Boolean value to specify if the user can change the margins and other page setup options available in print preview.

doc.summary: Returns a QueryTable object that represents the query table that intersects the specified Range object.
Returns or sets the reading order for the specified object. Can be one of the following XlReadingOrder constants: xlRTL (right-to-left), xlLTR (left-to-right), or xlContext. Read/write Long.
Removes duplicate values from a range of values.

params.name: Columns
params.text: Array of indexes of the columns that contain the duplicate information.

params.name: Header
params.text: Specifies whether the first row contains header information. xlNo is the default value; specify xlGuess if you want Excel to attempt to determine the header.

doc.summary: Removes subtotals from a list.
Clears the cell values of the cells in the range, with special consideration given to cell controls.
Returns a Range object that represents all the cells whose contents are different from those of the comparison cell in each row.

params.name: Comparison
params.text: A single cell to compare with the specified range.

members.name: Run
members.signature: Run([Arg1 As Variant], [Arg2 As Variant], [Arg3 As Variant], [Arg4 As Variant], [Arg5 As Variant], [Arg6 As Variant], [Arg7 As Variant], [Arg8 As Variant], [Arg9 As Variant], [Arg10 As Variant], [Arg11 As Variant], [Arg12 As Variant], [Arg13 As Variant], [Arg14 As Variant], [Arg15 As Variant], [Arg16 As Variant], [Arg17 As Variant], [Arg18 As Variant], [Arg19 As Variant], [Arg20 As Variant], [Arg21 As Variant], [Arg22 As Variant], [Arg23 As Variant], [Arg24 As Variant], [Arg25 As Variant], [Arg26 As Variant], [Arg27 As Variant], [Arg28 As Variant], [Arg29 As Variant], [Arg30 As Variant]) As Variant

doc.summary: Runs the Microsoft Excel macro at this location. The range must be on a macro sheet.

True if all of the cells in the range would be saved to file as an array formula; False if none of the cells in the range would be saved to file as a legacy array formula; null otherwise. Read-only Variant.
Specifies the actions that can be performed on the SharePoint server for a Range object.

Creates another instance of a Linked data type, such as Stocks or Geography, that exists in another cell. The new instance will be linked to the data source in the same way as the original, so it will refresh from the service if you call the Workbook.RefreshAll method.
Creates Phonetic objects for all the cells in the specified range.
Scrolls through the contents of the active window to move the range into view. The range must consist of a single cell in the active document.

For a cell containing a Linked data type, such as Stocks or Geography, this method causes a card to appear that shows details about the cell (that is, the same card that the user can view by choosing the cell icon).
Draws tracer arrows to the direct dependents of the range.

params.name: Remove
params.text: True to remove one level of tracer arrows to direct dependents. False to expand one level of tracer arrows. The default value is False.

doc.summary: True if the outline is expanded for the specified range (so that the detail of the column or row is visible). The specified range must be a single summary column or row in an outline. Read/write Variant.
Draws tracer arrows through the precedents tree to the cell that's the source of the error, and returns the range that contains that cell.
Draws tracer arrows to the direct precedents of the range.

params.name: Remove
params.text: True to remove one level of tracer arrows to direct precedents. False to expand one level of tracer arrows. The default value is False.

doc.summary: Returns or sets a Variant value that indicates if text automatically shrinks to fit in the available column width.

members.name: SortSpecial
members.signature: SortSpecial([SortMethod As XlSortMethod], [Key1 As Variant], [Order1 As XlSortOrder], [Type As Variant], [Key2 As Variant], [Order2 As XlSortOrder], [Key3 As Variant], [Order3 As XlSortOrder], [Header As XlYesNoGuess], [OrderCustom As Variant], [MatchCase As Variant], [Orientation As XlSortOrientation], [DataOption1 As XlSortDataOption], [DataOption2 As XlSortDataOption], [DataOption3 As XlSortDataOption]) As Variant

doc.summary: Uses East Asian sorting methods to sort the range, a PivotTable report, or uses the method for the active region if the range contains only one cell. For example, Japanese sorts in the order of the Kana syllabary.

params.name: SortMethod
params.text: The type of sort. Some of these constants may not be available to you, depending on the language support (U.S. English, for example) that you have selected or installed.

params.name: Key1
params.text: The first sort field, as either text (a PivotTable field or range name) or a Range object ("Dept" or Cells(1, 1), for example).

params.name: Order1
params.text: The sort order for the field or range specified in the _Key1_ argument.

params.name: Type
params.text: Specifies which elements are to be sorted. Use this argument only when sorting PivotTable reports.

params.name: Key2
params.text: The second sort field, as either text (a PivotTable field or range name) or a Range object. If you omit this argument, there's no second sort field. Cannot be used when sorting PivotTable reports.

params.name: Order2
params.text: The sort order for the field or range specified in the _Key2_ argument. Cannot be used when sorting PivotTable reports.

params.name: Key3
params.text: The third sort field, as either text (a range name) or a Range object. If you omit this argument, there's no third sort field. Cannot be used when sorting PivotTable reports.

params.name: Order3
params.text: The sort order for the field or range specified in the _Key3_ argument. Cannot be used when sorting PivotTable reports.

params.name: Header
params.text: Specifies whether or not the first row contains headers. Cannot be used when sorting PivotTable reports.

params.name: OrderCustom
params.text: This argument is a one-based integer offset to the list of custom sort orders. If you omit _OrderCustom_, (normal sort order) is used.

params.name: MatchCase
params.text: True to do a case-sensitive sort; False to do a sort that's not case-sensitive. Cannot be used when sorting PivotTable reports.

params.name: Orientation
params.text: The sort orientation.

params.name: DataOption1
params.text: Specifies how to sort text in _Key1_. Cannot be used when sorting PivotTable reports.

params.name: DataOption2
params.text: Specifies how to sort text in _Key2_. Cannot be used when sorting PivotTable reports.

params.name: DataOption3
params.text: Specifies how to sort text in _Key3_. Cannot be used when sorting PivotTable reports.

doc.summary: Causes the cells of the range to be spoken in row order or column order.

params.name: SpeakDirection
params.text: The speak direction, by rows or columns.

params.name: SpeakFormulas
params.text: True will cause formulas to be sent to the Text-To-Speech (TTS) engine for cells that have formulas. The value is sent if the cells don't have formulas. False (default) will cause values to always be sent to the TTS engine.

doc.summary: If a cell is a member of a spill, returns the cell containing the formula responsible. Otherwise, an error is returned.

members.name: SubscribeTo
members.signature: SubscribeTo(Edition As String, [Format As XlSubscribeToFormat]) As Variant

doc.summary: You have requested Help for a Visual Basic keyword used only on the Macintosh. For information about this keyword, consult the language reference Help included with Microsoft Office Macintosh Edition.

params.name: Edition
params.text: See help included with Microsoft Office Macintosh Edition.

params.name: Format
params.text: See help included with Microsoft Office Macintosh Edition.

members.name: Subtotal
members.signature: Subtotal(GroupBy As Long, Function As XlConsolidationFunction, TotalList As Variant, [Replace As Variant], [PageBreaks As Variant], [SummaryBelowData As XlSummaryRow]) As Variant

doc.summary: Creates subtotals for the range (or the current region, if the range is a single cell).

params.name: GroupBy
params.text: The field to group by, as a one-based integer offset. For more information, see the example.

params.name: Function
params.text: The subtotal function.

params.name: TotalList
params.text: An array of 1-based field offsets, indicating the fields to which the subtotals are added. For more information, see the example.

params.name: Replace
params.text: True to replace existing subtotals. The default value is True.

params.name: PageBreaks
params.text: True to add page breaks after each group. The default value is False.

params.name: SummaryBelowData
params.text: Places the summary data relative to the subtotal.

doc.summary: True if the range is an outlining summary row or column. The range should be a row or a column. Read-only Variant.

members.name: Table
members.signature: Table([RowInput As Variant], [ColumnInput As Variant]) As Variant

doc.summary: Creates a data table based on input values and formulas that you define on a worksheet.

params.name: RowInput
params.text: A single cell to use as the row input for your table.

params.name: ColumnInput
params.text: A single cell to use as the column input for your table.

doc.summary: Promotes a range in an outline (that is, decreases its outline level). The specified range must be a row or column, or a range of rows or columns. If the range is in a PivotTable report, this method ungroups the items contained in the range.
True if the row height of the Range object equals the standard height of the sheet. Returns Null if the range contains more than one row and the rows aren't all the same height. Read/write Variant.

True if the column width of the Range object equals the standard width of the sheet. Returns null if the range contains more than one column and the columns aren't all the same width. Read/write Variant.
When used without an object qualifier, this property returns an Application object that represents the Microsoft Excel application.
Returns a Long value that represents the number of objects in the collection.
Returns a 32-bit integer that indicates the application in which this object was created. Read-only Long.
Returns a single object from a collection.
Returns the parent object for the specified object. Read-only.
Creates a new workbook. The new workbook becomes the active workbook.

params.name: Template
params.text: Determines how the new workbook is created. If this argument is a string specifying the name of an existing Microsoft Excel file, the new workbook is created with the specified file as a template. If this argument is a constant, the new workbook contains a single sheet of the specified type. Can be one of the following XlWBATemplate constants: xlWBATChart, xlWBATExcel4IntlMacroSheet, xlWBATExcel4MacroSheet, or xlWBATWorksheet. If this argument is omitted, Microsoft Excel creates a new workbook with a number of blank sheets (the number of sheets is set by the SheetsInNewWorkbook property).

doc.summary: Closes the object.

members.name: Open
members.signature: Open(Filename As String, [UpdateLinks As Variant], [ReadOnly As Variant], [Format As Variant], [Password As Variant], [WriteResPassword As Variant], [IgnoreReadOnlyRecommended As Variant], [Origin As Variant], [Delimiter As Variant], [Editable As Variant], [Notify As Variant], [Converter As Variant], [AddToMru As Variant], [Local As Variant], [CorruptLoad As Variant]) As Workbook

doc.summary: Opens a workbook.

params.name: Filename
params.text: String. The file name of the workbook to be opened.

params.name: UpdateLinks
params.text: Specifies the way external references (links) in the file, such as the reference to a range in the Budget.xls workbook in the following formula =SUM([Budget.xls]Annual!C10:C25), are updated. If this argument is omitted, the user is prompted to specify how links will be updated. For more information about the values used by this parameter, see the Remarks section. If Microsoft Excel is opening a file in the WKS, WK1, or WK3 format and the UpdateLinks argument is 0, no charts are created; otherwise, Microsoft Excel generates charts from the graphs attached to the file.

params.name: ReadOnly
params.text: True to open the workbook in read-only mode.

params.name: Format
params.text: If Microsoft Excel opens a text file, this argument specifies the delimiter character. If this argument is omitted, the current delimiter is used. For more information about the values used by this parameter, see the Remarks section.

params.name: Password
params.text: A string that contains the password required to open a protected workbook. If this argument is omitted and the workbook requires a password, the user is prompted for the password.

params.name: WriteResPassword
params.text: A string that contains the password required to write to a write-reserved workbook. If this argument is omitted and the workbook requires a password, the user will be prompted for the password.

params.name: IgnoreReadOnlyRecommended
params.text: True to have Microsoft Excel not display the read-only recommended message (if the workbook was saved with the Read-Only Recommended option).

params.name: Origin
params.text: If the file is a text file, this argument indicates where it originated, so that code pages and Carriage Return/Line Feed (CR/LF) can be mapped correctly. Can be one of the following XlPlatform constants: xlMacintosh, xlWindows, or xlMSDOS. If this argument is omitted, the current operating system is used.

params.name: Delimiter
params.text: If the file is a text file and the _Format_ argument is 6, this argument is a string that specifies the character to be used as the delimiter. For example, use Chr(9) for tabs, use "," for commas, use ";" for semicolons, or use a custom character. Only the first character of the string is used.

params.name: Editable
params.text: If the file is a Microsoft Excel 4.0 add-in, this argument is True to open the add-in so that it is a visible window. If this argument is False or omitted, the add-in is opened as hidden, and it cannot be unhidden. This option does not apply to add-ins created in Microsoft Excel 5.0 or later. If the file is an Excel template, True to open the specified template for editing. False to open a new workbook based on the specified template. The default value is False.

params.name: Notify
params.text: If the file cannot be opened in read/write mode, this argument is True to add the file to the file notification list. Microsoft Excel will open the file as read-only, poll the file notification list, and then notify the user when the file becomes available. If this argument is False or omitted, no notification is requested, and any attempts to open an unavailable file will fail.

params.name: Converter
params.text: The index of the first file converter to try when opening the file. The specified file converter is tried first; if this converter does not recognize the file, all other converters are tried. The converter index consists of the row numbers of the converters returned by the FileConverters property.

params.name: AddToMru
params.text: True to add this workbook to the list of recently used files. The default value is False.

params.name: Local
params.text: True saves files against the language of Microsoft Excel (including control panel settings). False (default) saves files against the language of Visual Basic for Applications (VBA) (which is typically United States English unless the VBA project where Workbooks.Open is run from is an old internationalized XL5/95 VBA project).

params.name: CorruptLoad
params.text: Can be one of the following constants: xlNormalLoad, xlRepairFile and xlExtractData. The default behavior if no value is specified is xlNormalLoad, and does not attempt recovery when initiated through the OM.

members.name: OpenDatabase
members.signature: OpenDatabase(Filename As String, [CommandText As Variant], [CommandType As Variant], [BackgroundQuery As Variant], [ImportDataAs As Variant]) As Workbook

doc.summary: Returns a Workbook object representing a database.

params.name: Filename
params.text: The connection string that contains the location and file name of the database.

params.name: CommandText
params.text: The command text of the query.

params.name: CommandType
params.text: The command type of the query. Specify one of the constants of the XlCmdType enumeration: xlCmdCube, xlCmdList, xlCmdSql, xlCmdTable, and xlCmdDefault.

params.name: BackgroundQuery
params.text: This parameter is a variant data type but you can only pass a Boolean value. If you pass True, the query is performed in the background (asynchronously). The default value is False.

params.name: ImportDataAs
params.text: This parameter uses one of the values of the XlImportDataAs enumeration. The two values of this enum are xlPivotTableReport and xlQueryTable. Pass one of these values to return the data as a PivotTable or QueryTable. The default value is xlQueryTable.

members.name: OpenText
members.signature: OpenText(Filename As String, [Origin As Variant], [StartRow As Variant], [DataType As Variant], [TextQualifier As XlTextQualifier], [ConsecutiveDelimiter As Variant], [Tab As Variant], [Semicolon As Variant], [Comma As Variant], [Space As Variant], [Other As Variant], [OtherChar As Variant], [FieldInfo As Variant], [TextVisualLayout As Variant], [DecimalSeparator As Variant], [ThousandsSeparator As Variant], [TrailingMinusNumbers As Variant], [Local As Variant])

doc.summary: Loads and parses a text file as a new workbook with a single sheet that contains the parsed text-file data.

params.name: Filename
params.text: Specifies the file name of the text file to be opened and parsed.

params.name: Origin
params.text: Specifies the origin of the text file. Can be one of the following XlPlatform constants: xlMacintosh, xlWindows, or xlMSDOS. Additionally, this could be an integer representing the code page number of the desired code page. For example, "1256" would specify that the encoding of the source text file is Arabic (Windows). If this argument is omitted, the method uses the current setting of the File Origin option in the Text Import Wizard.

params.name: StartRow
params.text: The row number at which to start parsing text. The default value is 1.

params.name: DataType
params.text: Specifies the column format of the data in the file. Can be one of the following XlTextParsingType constants: xlDelimited or xlFixedWidth. If this argument is not specified, Microsoft Excel attempts to determine the column format when it opens the file.

params.name: TextQualifier
params.text: Specifies the text qualifier.

params.name: ConsecutiveDelimiter
params.text: True to have consecutive delimiters considered one delimiter. The default is False.

params.name: Tab
params.text: True to have the tab character be the delimiter (DataType must be xlDelimited). The default value is False.

params.name: Semicolon
params.text: True to have the semicolon character be the delimiter (DataType must be xlDelimited). The default value is False.

params.name: Comma
params.text: True to have the comma character be the delimiter (DataType must be xlDelimited). The default value is False.

params.name: Space
params.text: True to have the space character be the delimiter (DataType must be xlDelimited). The default value is False.

params.name: Other
params.text: True to have the character specified by the _OtherChar_ argument be the delimiter (DataType must be xlDelimited). The default value is False.

params.name: OtherChar
params.text: Required if _Other_ is True. Specifies the delimiter character when _Other_ is True. If more than one character is specified, only the first character of the string is used; the remaining characters are ignored.

params.name: FieldInfo
params.text: An array containing parse information for individual columns of data. The interpretation depends on the value of _DataType_. When the data is delimited, this argument is an array of two-element arrays, with each two-element array specifying the conversion options for a particular column. The first element is the column number (1-based), and the second element is one of the XlColumnDataType constants specifying how the column is parsed.

params.name: TextVisualLayout
params.text: The visual layout of the text.

params.name: DecimalSeparator
params.text: The decimal separator that Microsoft Excel uses when recognizing numbers. The default setting is the system setting.

params.name: ThousandsSeparator
params.text: The thousands separator that Excel uses when recognizing numbers. The default setting is the system setting.

params.name: TrailingMinusNumbers
params.text: Specify True if numbers with a minus character at the end should be treated as negative numbers. If False or omitted, numbers with a minus character at the end are treated as text.

params.name: Local
params.text: Specify True if regional settings of the machine should be used for separators, numbers and data formatting.

members.name: OpenXML
members.signature: OpenXML(Filename As String, [Stylesheets As Variant], [LoadOption As Variant]) As Workbook

doc.summary: Opens an XML data file. Returns a Workbook object.

params.name: Filename
params.text: The name of the file to open.

params.name: Stylesheets
params.text: Either a single value or an array of values that specify which XSL Transformation (XSLT) stylesheet processing instructions to apply.

params.name: LoadOption
params.text: Specifies how Excel opens the XML data file. Can be one of the XlXmlLoadOption constants.

doc.summary: True if Microsoft Excel can check out a specified workbook from a server. Read/write Boolean.

params.name: Filename
params.text: The name of the file to check out.

doc.summary: Returns a String representing a specified workbook from a server to a local computer for editing.

params.name: Filename
params.text: The name of the file to check out.

doc.summary: When used without an object qualifier, this property returns an Application object that represents the Microsoft Excel application.
Returns a Long value that represents the number of objects in the collection.
Returns a 32-bit integer that indicates the application in which this object was created. Read-only Long.
Returns a single object from a collection.
Returns the parent object for the specified object. Read-only.
Returns or sets a Variant value that determines whether the object is visible.

members.name: Add
members.signature: Add([Before As Variant], [After As Variant], [Count As Variant], [Type As Variant]) As Object

doc.summary: Creates a new worksheet, chart, or macro sheet. The new worksheet becomes the active sheet.

params.name: Before
params.text: An object that specifies the sheet before which the new sheet is added.

params.name: After
params.text: An object that specifies the sheet after which the new sheet is added.

params.name: Count
params.text: The number of sheets to be added. The default value is one.

params.name: Type
params.text: Specifies the sheet type. Can be one of the following XlSheetType constants: xlWorksheet, xlChart, xlExcel4MacroSheet, or xlExcel4IntlMacroSheet. If you are inserting a sheet based on an existing template, specify the path to the template. The default value is xlWorksheet.

members.name: Add2
members.signature: Add2([Before As Variant], [After As Variant], [Count As Variant], [NewLayout As Variant]) As Object

doc.summary: This method is only implemented for the Charts collection object and will produce a run-time error if used on the Sheets and Worksheets objects.

params.name: Before
params.text: An object that specifies the sheet before which the new sheet is added.

params.name: After
params.text: An object that specifies the sheet after which the new sheet is added.

params.name: Count
params.text: The number of sheets to be added. The default value is one.

params.name: NewLayout
params.text: The layout of the new worksheet.

doc.summary: Copies the sheet to another location in the workbook.

params.name: Before
params.text: The sheet before which the copied sheet will be placed. You cannot specify _Before_ if you specify _After_.

params.name: After

members.name: PrintOut

params.name: From

params.name: Copies
params.text: The number of copies to print. If this argument is omitted, one copy is printed.

params.name: Preview
params.text: True to have Microsoft Excel invoke print preview before printing the object. False (or omitted) to print the object immediately.

params.text: Sets the name of the active printer.

params.text: True to print to a file. If _PrToFileName_ is not specified, Microsoft Excel prompts the user to enter the name of the output file.

params.name: Collate
params.text: True to collate multiple copies.

params.name: PrToFileName
params.text: If _PrintToFile_ is set to True, this argument specifies the name of the file that you want to print to.

params.name: IgnorePrintAreas
params.text: True to ignore print areas and print the entire object.

doc.summary: Shows a preview of the object as it would look when printed.

params.name: EnableChanges
params.text: Pass a Boolean value to specify if the user can change the margins and other page setup options available in print preview.

doc.summary: Selects the object.

params.text: Used only with sheets. True to replace the current selection with the specified object. False to extend the current selection to include any previously selected objects and the specified object.

members.name: Close
members.signature: Close([SaveChanges As Variant], [Filename As Variant], [RouteWorkbook As Variant]) As Boolean

doc.summary: Closes the object.

params.name: SaveChanges
params.text: If there are no changes to the workbook, this argument is ignored. If there are changes to the workbook and the workbook appears in other open windows, this argument is ignored. If there are changes to the workbook but the workbook doesn't appear in any other open windows, this argument specifies whether changes should be saved.If set to True, changes are saved to the workbook. If there is not yet a file name associated with the workbook, _FileName_ is used. If _FileName_ is omitted, the user is asked to supply a file name.

params.name: Filename
params.text: Save changes under this file name.

params.name: RouteWorkbook
params.text: If the workbook doesn't need to be routed to the next recipient (if it has no routing slip or has already been routed), this argument is ignored. Otherwise, Microsoft Excel routes the workbook according to the value of this parameter. If set to True, the workbook is sent to the next recipient. If set to False, the workbook is not sent. If omitted, the user is asked whether the workbook should be sent.

members.name: Add
members.signature: Add([Name As Variant], [RefersTo As Variant], [Visible As Variant], [MacroType As Variant], [ShortcutKey As Variant], [Category As Variant], [NameLocal As Variant], [RefersToLocal As Variant], [CategoryLocal As Variant], [RefersToR1C1 As Variant], [RefersToR1C1Local As Variant]) As Name

doc.summary: Defines a new name for a range of cells.

params.name: Name
params.text: Specifies the text, in English, to use as the name if the _NameLocal_ parameter is not specified. Names cannot include spaces and cannot be formatted as cell references.

params.name: RefersTo
params.text: Describes what the name refers to, in English, using A1-style notation, if the _RefersToLocal_, _RefersToR1C1_, and _RefersToR1C1Local_ parameters are not specified. NOTE: Nothing is returned if the reference does not exist.

params.name: Visible
params.text: True specifies that the name is defined as visible. False specifies that the name is defined as hidden. A hidden name does not appear in the Define Name, Paste Name, or Goto dialog box. The default value is True.

params.name: MacroType
params.text:
The macro type, determined by one of the following values:

  • 1 - User-defined function (Function procedure)

  • 2 - Macro (Sub procedure)

  • 3 or omitted - None (the name does not refer to a user-defined function or macro)

params.name: ShortcutKey
params.text: Specifies the macro shortcut key. Must be a single letter, such as "z" or "Z". Applies only for command macros.

params.name: Category
params.text: The category of the macro or function if the _MacroType_ argument equals 1 or 2. The category is used in the Function Wizard. Existing categories can be referred to either by number, starting at 1, or by name, in English. Excel creates a new category if the specified category does not exist.

params.name: NameLocal
params.text: Specifies the localized text to use as the name if the _Name_ parameter is not specified. Names cannot include spaces and cannot be formatted as cell references.

params.name: RefersToLocal
params.text: Describes what the name refers to, in localized text using A1-style notation, if the _RefersTo_, _RefersToR1C1_, and _RefersToR1C1Local_ parameters are not specified.

params.name: CategoryLocal
params.text: Specifies the localized text that identifies the category of a custom function if the _Category_ parameter is not specified.

params.name: RefersToR1C1
params.text: Describes what the name refers to, in English using R1C1-style notation, if the _RefersTo_, _RefersToLocal_, and _RefersToR1C1Local_ parameters are not specified.

params.name: RefersToR1C1Local
params.text: Describes what the name refers to, in localized text using R1C1-style notation, if the _RefersTo_, _RefersToLocal_, and _RefersToR1C1_ parameters are not specified.

members.name: Item
members.signature: Item([Index As Variant], [IndexLocal As Variant], [RefersTo As Variant]) As Name

params.name: Index

params.name: RefersTo
params.text: What the name refers to. You use this argument to identify a name by what it refers to.

members.name: Add
members.signature: Add([SourceType As XlListObjectSourceType], [Source As Variant], [LinkSource As Variant], [XlListObjectHasHeaders As XlYesNoGuess], [Destination As Variant], [TableStyleName As Variant]) As ListObject

doc.summary: Creates a new list object.

params.name: SourceType
params.text: Indicates the kind of source for the query.

params.name: Source
params.text:
When _SourceType_ = xlSrcRange: A Range object representing the data source. If omitted, the _Source_ will default to the range returned by list range detection code. When _SourceType_ = xlSrcExternal: An array of String values specifying a connection to the source, containing the following elements:
  • 0 - URL to SharePoint site
  • 1 - ListName
  • 2 - ViewGUID
When _SourceType_ = xlSrcQuery: Accepts ODBC or OLEDB connection string (this also works with xlSrcExternal). When _SourceType_ = xlSrcModel: Accepts a WorkbookConnection object (see TableObject for example).

params.name: LinkSource
params.text: Indicates whether an external data source is to be linked to the ListObject object. If _SourceType_ is xlSrcExternal, the default is True. Invalid if _SourceType_ is xlSrcRange, and will return an error if not omitted.

params.name: XlListObjectHasHeaders
params.text: An XlYesNoGuess constant that indicates whether the data being imported has column labels. If the _Source_ does not contain headers, Excel will automatically generate headers. Default value: xlGuess.

params.name: Destination
params.text: A Range object specifying a single-cell reference as the destination for the top-left corner of the new list object. If the Range object refers to more than one cell, an error is generated. The _Destination_ argument must be specified when _SourceType_ is set to xlSrcExternal. The _Destination_ argument is ignored if _SourceType_ is set to xlSrcRange. The destination range must be on the worksheet that contains the ListObjects collection specified by _expression_. New columns will be inserted at the _Destination_ to fit the new list. Therefore, existing data will not be overwritten.

params.name: TableStyleName
params.text: The name of a TableStyle; for example "TableStyleLight1".

If True, the cells below the table will be shifted down one row. If False, if the row below the table is empty, the table will expand to occupy that row without shifting cells below it, but if the row below the table contains data, those cells will be shifted down when the new row is inserted.

members.name: ChartWizard
members.signature: ChartWizard([Source As Variant], [Gallery As Variant], [Format As Variant], [PlotBy As Variant], [CategoryLabels As Variant], [SeriesLabels As Variant], [HasLegend As Variant], [Title As Variant], [CategoryTitle As Variant], [ValueTitle As Variant], [ExtraTitle As Variant])

doc.summary: Modifies the properties of the given chart. Use this method to quickly format a chart without setting all the individual properties. This method is noninteractive, and it changes only the specified properties.

params.name: Source
params.text: The range that contains the source data for the new chart. If this argument is omitted, Microsoft Excel edits the active chart sheet or the selected chart on the active worksheet.

params.name: Gallery
params.text: One of the constants of XlChartType specifying the chart type.

params.name: Format
params.text: The option number for the built-in autoformats. Can be a number from 1 through 10, depending on the gallery type. If this argument is omitted, Excel chooses a default value based on the gallery type and data source.

params.name: PlotBy
params.text: Specifies whether the data for each series is in rows or columns. Can be one of the following XlRowCol constants: xlRows or xlColumns.

params.name: CategoryLabels
params.text: An integer specifying the number of rows or columns within the source range that contain category labels. Legal values are from 0 (zero) through one less than the maximum number of the corresponding categories or series.

params.name: SeriesLabels
params.text: An integer specifying the number of rows or columns within the source range that contain series labels. Legal values are from 0 (zero) through one less than the maximum number of the corresponding categories or series.

params.name: HasLegend
params.text: True to include a legend.

params.name: Title
params.text: The chart title text.

params.name: CategoryTitle
params.text: The category axis title text.

params.name: ValueTitle
params.text: The value axis title text.

params.name: ExtraTitle
params.text: The series axis title for 3D charts or the second value axis title for 2D charts.

doc.summary: Copies the sheet to another location in the workbook.

params.name: Before
params.text: The sheet before which the copied sheet will be placed. You cannot specify _Before_ if you specify _After_.

params.name: After

members.name: PrintOut
members.signature: PrintOut([From As Variant], [To As Variant], [Copies As Variant], [Preview As Variant], [ActivePrinter As Variant], [PrintToFile As Variant], [Collate As Variant], [PrToFileName As Variant])

doc.summary: Prints the object.

params.name: From
params.text: The number of the page at which to start printing. If this argument is omitted, printing starts at the beginning.

params.name: To

members.name: Protect
members.signature: Protect([Password As Variant], [DrawingObjects As Variant], [Contents As Variant], [Scenarios As Variant], [UserInterfaceOnly As Variant])

doc.summary: Protects a chart so that it cannot be modified.

params.name: Password
params.text: A string that specifies a case-sensitive password for the worksheet or workbook. If this argument is omitted, you can unprotect the worksheet or workbook without using a password. Otherwise, you must specify the password to unprotect the worksheet or workbook. If you forget the password, you cannot unprotect the worksheet or workbook. Use strong passwords that combine uppercase and lowercase letters, numbers, and symbols. Weak passwords don't mix these elements. Strong password: Y6dh!et5. Weak password: House27. Passwords should be 8 or more characters in length. A pass phrase that uses 14 or more characters is better. It's critical that you remember your password. If you forget your password, Microsoft cannot retrieve it. Store the passwords that you write down in a secure place away from the information that they help protect.

params.name: DrawingObjects
params.text: True to protect shapes. The default value is True.

params.name: Contents
params.text: True to protect contents. For a chart, this protects the entire chart. For a worksheet, this protects the locked cells. The default value is True.

params.name: Scenarios
params.text: True to protect scenarios. This argument is valid only for worksheets. The default value is True.

params.name: UserInterfaceOnly
params.text: True to protect the user interface, but not macros. If this argument is omitted, protection applies both to macros and to the user interface.

doc.summary: True if the contents of the sheet are protected. For a chart, this protects the entire chart. To turn on content protection, use the Protect method with the _Contents_ argument set to True. Read-only Boolean.
True if series formulas cannot be modified by the user. Read/write Boolean.
True if shapes are protected. To turn on shape protection, use the Protect method with the _DrawingObjects_ argument set to True. Read-only Boolean.
True if chart formatting cannot be modified by the user. Read/write Boolean.
True if user-interface-only protection is turned on. To turn on user interface protection, use the Protect method with the _UserInterfaceOnly_ argument set to True. Read-only Boolean.
True if chart elements cannot be selected. Read/write Boolean.
True if the chart axes are at right angles, independent of chart rotation or elevation. Applies only to 3D line, column, and bar charts. Read/write Boolean.

Returns or sets the rotation of the 3D chart view (the rotation of the plot area around the z-axis, in degrees). The value of this property must be from 0 to 360, except for 3D bar charts, where the value must be from 0 to 44. The default value is 20. Applies only to 3D charts. Read/write Variant.

members.name: SaveAs
members.signature: SaveAs(Filename As String, [FileFormat As Variant], [Password As Variant], [WriteResPassword As Variant], [ReadOnlyRecommended As Variant], [CreateBackup As Variant], [AddToMru As Variant], [TextCodepage As Variant], [TextVisualLayout As Variant], [Local As Variant])

doc.summary: Saves changes to the chart or worksheet in a different file.

params.name: Filename
params.text: Variant. A string that indicates the name of the file to be saved. You can include a full path; if you don't, Microsoft Excel saves the file in the current folder.

params.name: FileFormat
params.text: The file format to use when you save the file. For a list of valid choices, see the FileFormat property. For an existing file, the default format is the last file format specified; for a new file, the default is the format of the version of Excel being used.

params.name: Password
params.text: A case-sensitive string (no more than 15 characters) that indicates the protection password to be given to the file.

params.name: WriteResPassword
params.text: A string that indicates the write-reservation password for this file. If a file is saved with the password and the password isn't supplied when the file is opened, the file is opened as read-only.

params.name: ReadOnlyRecommended
params.text: True to display a message when the file is opened, recommending that the file be opened as read-only.

params.name: CreateBackup
params.text: True to create a backup file.

params.name: AddToMru
params.text: True to add this workbook to the list of recently used files. The default value is False.

params.name: TextCodepage
params.text: Not used in U.S. English Microsoft Excel.

params.name: TextVisualLayout
params.text: Not used in U.S. English Microsoft Excel.

params.name: Local
params.text: True saves files against the language of Microsoft Excel (including control panel settings). False (default) saves files against the language of Visual Basic for Applications (VBA) (which is typically US English unless the VBA project where Workbooks.Open is run from is an old internationalized XL5/95 VBA project).
